compiled_state.title: Add deleted-tombstone blocker test to test_ticket_graph.py
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
`_get_ticket_status()` in `_status.py:32` reads `.tombstone.json` and returns `tombstone.get("status", "closed")`. Writing `.tombstone.json` with `{"status": "deleted"}` makes `_get_ticket_status` return `"deleted"`. The current `_graph.py` blocker check (`status != "closed"`) treats "deleted" as non-terminal, so blocked tickets never become ready_to_work when their blocker is deleted.

## What to do
Add a test function to `tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py` that:
1. Creates two tickets: A (blocked by B)
2. Writes `.tombstone.json` with `{"status": "deleted"}` into B's ticket directory (simulating what ticket-delete will do)
3. Calls `get_ticket_graph()` and asserts `ready_to_work=True` for ticket A

## Files to modify
- `tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py` — add test function

## Acceptance criteria
- New test function named `test_ready_to_work_when_blocker_has_deleted_tombstone` (or similar)
- Uses the existing test fixture pattern (in-memory or temp git repo with the worktree fixture helpers)
- Writes `.tombstone.json` with `{"status": "deleted"}` into the blocker's ticket directory
- Asserts `ready_to_work=True` in graph output for the blocked ticket
- Test FAILS RED before T2 (because current `_graph.py` has `status != "closed"` — "deleted" != "closed" → `ready_to_work=False`)
- Test passes GREEN after T2's one-line fix is applied

## Testing mode
UPDATE — adding a test for existing behavior that needs to change

comments.body: I don't see a ticket description in your message. Could you please provide the ticket content? It should describe what changes need to be made, which will help me identify which source and test files are most likely to be modified.
comments.author: Test
